_description: Acute STEMI emergency — inferior wall. Primary PTCA planned. Tests emergency flag (Phase 1 stub returns non-emergency), STG check with ECG + troponin evidence, cardiology package matching.

clinical.chief_complaints: Crushing central chest pain radiating to left arm and jaw for 4 hours, profuse sweating, breathlessness, nausea
clinical.history_of_present_illness: Patient was apparently well until 4 hours ago when he developed sudden onset crushing central chest pain radiating to left arm and jaw, associated with profuse diaphoresis, breathlessness, and nausea. No prior cardiac history. No prior hospitalisations. Currently haemodynamically unstable on arrival. Family brought him to ER.

examination_findings.general: Patient conscious, restless, diaphoretic, pallor present
examination_findings.cvs: S1 S2 heard, no murmurs, JVP elevated, peripheral pulses weak and thready
examination_findings.rs: Bilateral basal crackles present, no wheeze

investigations.result_summary: ST elevation 2-3mm in leads II, III, aVF. Reciprocal ST depression in I, aVL. Heart rate 115/min. Sinus tachycardia. Features consistent with acute inferior wall STEMI.
Troponin I: 2.8 ng/mL (elevated, ref <0.04), CK-MB: 48 U/L (elevated), Hb: 12.4 g/dL, WBC: 11200/cumm, creatinine: 1.1 mg/dL, blood glucose: 210 mg/dL

clinical.past_medical_history: Hypertension 10 years, Type 2 DM 5 years on oral medication. No prior cardiac events.

personal_history.smoking: ex-smoker, 20 pack years, quit 5 years ago

non_clinical_documents_in_hand.label: Photo of patient on hospital bed

clinical.notes: Patient in cardiogenic shock. IV fluids, oxygen, aspirin loading dose, IV heparin given. Cath lab activated for primary PTCA. Consent obtained from family.
